snippet.title: 2 Girls for 1 Mike Ross | Suits
snippet.description:
Mike's (Patrick J. Adam) empathy for an executive Harvey (Gabriel Macht) fired on behalf of a client causes him to question the motives for their dismissal.

Season 01, Episode 10, Shelf Life,
Mike's (Patrick J. Adam) empathy for an executive Harvey (Gabriel Macht) fired on behalf of a client causes him to question the motives for their dismissal. When Mike’s actions jeopardize the firm's relationship with the client, Harvey must keep him out of Jessica's (Gina Torres) line of fire. As Mike and Jenny (Vanessa Ray) grow closer, Rachel (Meghan Markle) realizes her feelings for Mike are stronger than she thought.


All 9 seasons of Suits streaming only on @Peacock: https://pck.tv/46kqJxO

Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCoSwRgn1R8fPBNI-c4yjWUw?sub_confirmation=1

Suits delves into the fast-paced, high-stakes world of a top Manhattan corporate law firm where hotshot associate Harvey Specter (Gabriel Macht, “Love & Other Drugs”) makes a risky move by hiring Mike Ross (Patrick J. Adams, “Old School”) a brilliant but unmotivated college dropout, as his associate. As he becomes enmeshed in this unfamiliar world, Mike relies heavily on the firm’s best paralegal Rachel (Megan Markle, “Horrible Bosses”) and Harvey's no-nonsense assistant Donna (Sarah Rafferty, “Brothers & Sisters”) to help him serve justice. With a photographic memory and the street smarts of a hustler, Mike proves to be a legal prodigy despite the absence of bonafide legal credentials.

#PeacockTV #SuitsTV
 

Welcome to the official Suits YouTube channel: home to all the swagger, smarts and sexiness from Pearson Hardman - one of the top New York law firms that hire solely from Harvard law. Well, almost entirely. 

When Mike Ross, a crazy genius whose found himself on a bad path, stumbles into an interview with Harvey Specter he quickly talks his way into a job. You know the rest....

If you want to relive the best moments of Harvey Specter and Jessica Pearson, the funniest Donna scenes, the will they won't they romance of Mike Ross and Rachel.... or if you just want to get LIT UP subscribe to the Suits official YouTube channel.

snippet.title: The Future of Dentistry for Dr. Lynne Thomas
snippet.description:
The future of digital dentistry is in full force for Dr. Lynne Thomas. Watch as she takes us through a recent same-day crown case using her CEREC Primescan® and CEREC® Primemill — and how this technology seamlessly integrates with her cone beam computed tomography (CBCT) unit to assess patients’ temporomandibular joints, airway space and more.

snippet.title: ChatGPT and the Future of Dentistry
snippet.description:
Link to the Full Article: https://dentalresourceasia.com/chatgpt-and-the-future-of-dentistry/

Link to the Dental Resource Asia Website: https://dentalresourceasia.com

ChatGPT and the Future of Dentistry

ChatGPT’s impact on dentistry is more than just another disruptive tech.

In November 2022, the world of technology was introduced to ChatGPT, an artificial intelligence powered chatbot.

Given the dental profession has already been impacted by many technological advancements in recent years: From digital x-rays to automated appointment scheduling – you would think that disruptive technologies are nothing new. Well, not really.

ChatGPT is Not Just Another Disruptive Tech

over a million users flocked to the platform. Within just five days of its launch,

Scott Brinker, the highly regarded US-based editor-in-chief of chiefmartech.com described the platform’s success as a
 “pivotal ah-ha moment” in which the wider public became aware of the disruptive force of generative AI.

Brinker warns that we must be prepared for another wave of marketing technology innovation as the economy recovers,  as the exponential growth in this field is likely to continue.

Question Time

Around ChatGPT’s growing buzz, questions abound as to the outsized role 
 that this technology will play in our profession and industry:

How can ChatGPT support dentists in providing more efficient and personalized care to  patients? How can ChatGPT help dental practices streamline their administrative tasks? How can the technology be used to provide education and information to patients?

To fully grasp the capabilities and limitations of this technology, we must explore what it excels at and what it wasn’t designed to do.

So What the Heck is ChatGPT? ChatGPT is a language model designed to generate human-like responses to text inputs 

While ChatGPT is highly skilled in generating text that resembles human language,  it is not programmed to perform tasks that require physical action, complex reasoning, or decision-making.

Yet despite its limitations, ChatGPT has the potential to revolutionize various industries and streamline many tasks previously performed by humans.

Current AI Dental Applications

ChatGPT: Practical Applications for the Dental Practice

ChatGPT could streamline administrative tasks such as appointment scheduling and insurance claims processing,  freeing up staff to focus on more important tasks such as patient care.

The model could also generate detailed and accurate patient records, improving the quality of care provided by dental practices.

In patient education, ChatGPT has the potential to reduce confusion and anxiety for patients  by answering their questions and providing accurate, informative responses.

Tele-dentistry is
another area where ChatGPT could have a major impact. The model could assist with virtual consultations,  allowing dental professionals to answer patients’ questions and provide accurate information.

Certainly, there are broader ethical and privacy concerns that need to be addressed when using ChatGPT in the context of dental care   including ensuring patient data security,  and ensuring that the technology is not used to make clinical decisions without proper oversight. It is therefore imperative that dental and healthcare professionals consider how the technology might be used in a responsible, ethical and transparent manner.

Link to the Full Article: https://dentalresourceasia.com/chatgpt-and-the-future-of-dentistry/

Link to the Dental Resource Asia Website: https://dentalresourceasia.com

snippet.title: Kyle West Interview: Gene Griffin Slapping Andre Harrell Changed Shock Us at Uptown (Part 6)
snippet.description:
Join this channel to get access to perks:
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCZZqHU140sZn6oOuZVri1bg/join

Kyle Albert West, is a Multi-Platinum Record Producer (with 11 hits), Composer, Writer, and Keyboardist. Born on July 2, 1969 in Bronx, NY. West spent his earliest childhood years in The Bronx, prior to moving to Mount Vernon, NY at age nine. High school students Kyle West and Cousin Al B. Sure! started making music together, in West’s home basement. Kyle West attended Louisiana State University (LSU) majoring in accounting, when Eddie Ferrell introduced West to Andre Harrell of Uptown Entertainment in 1987. #KyleWest #albsure #newjackswing 
College Student, Kyle West started making demos with Al B.Sure! and in 1988 produced Al B.Sure’s! triple platinum debut album “In Effect Mode”. That released on May 3, 1988 as a joint venture between Uptown Records and Warner Bros. Records, “In Effect Mode” was #20 on the Billboard 200 ,#1 on the Top R&B/Hip-Hop Albums Chart (remaining for seven weeks), #7 on the Billboard Hot 100 Charts, and #1 on the Billboard Hot Black Singles Chart  
Al B. Sure! co-producer Kyle West recalls the making of In Effect Mode (1988) | Return To The Classics Kyle West was signed to Uptown Management in 1988, worked on remixes for artists such as Rod Stewart, Robert Palmer, Eddie Murphy, Rick James, and the Reverend Al Green, producing the Grammy Award-winning track “As Long As We’re Together” in 1989 for Green. West, also produced/worked on records with recording artists such as Freddie Jackson, Pebbles, K-Ci & Jojo from Jodeci, Tricia Covington, Tevin Campbell, [[[I Don't (Mariah Carey song) |Mariah Carey]]]], and Donell Jones. 

Five singles released from the 1988 album (West Produced),“In Effect Mode”, made the Billboard singles charts. "Off on Your Own (Girl)," "Rescue Me," "If I'm Not Your Lover" "Killing Me Softly", and "Nite and Day.” (There was a French-language version of "Night and Day" ("Nuit et Jour") also recorded, appearing on the 12-inch maxi-single of the original.) 
In 1991 West, along with cousin Sure! worked on Tevin Campbell’s debut album titled, “T.E.V.I.N” which produced the hit singles “Just Ask Me To” ,“Goodbye”, and “Alone With You”, that was #1 on the Billboard R&B Charts. Friend Eddie Ferrell (of Ferrell’s Untouchables Label) introduced West to Donell Jones, in 2000. Where Kyle West Co-Produced “Where I Wanna Be” with Jones, a track that was number 29 on the Hot 100 chart and number two on the R&B chart.

Hosted by Broadcaster & Psychotherapist Nnamdi E.S. Okoye, Halftime Chat R&B Podcast is your home for RnB, New Jack Swing and Old School Hip Hop. Dropping  Interviews with your favourite R&B artists & Music videos from the 80s, 90s and 2000s.

snippet.title: Victoria Rowell Interview - Y&R 50th Anniversary Party
snippet.description:
Victoria Rowell (ex-Drucilla) of The Young and the Restless chatted with Michael Fairman on the red carpet at the Y&R's 50th anniversary party.

During the conversation, Victoria recalls how the foster care storyline which brought Bryton James to the series as Devon, was her idea, working alongside the late Kristoff St. John (ex-Neil), and how Y&R portrayed and integrated an African-American family into the Genoa City canvas.

snippet.title: Dr. David Steiner on Teaching Wisdom in Schools
snippet.description: This week on The Learning Curve, guest cohosts Charlie Chieppo and Alisha Searcy join Dr. David Steiner for a wide-ranging discussion about the importance of education as a means of transmitting enduring wisdom to young people. Dr. Steiner discusses differences in K-12 education between the U.S. and the U.K., explores how schools of education may be contributing to the decline of K-12 education, reflects on the politicization of U.S. history and civics education, and talks about what states, governors, and state legislatures can do to lead systemic academic improvements. Dr. Steiner concludes the interview with a reading from his new book A Nation at Thought: Restoring Wisdom in America’s Schools.

Herritt, Director of International Student and Scholar Services at SUNY Buffalo State, shares her insights on how they approach international student recruitment and enrollment, with a focus on diversifying their student population. With the challenges of limited travel and expenses, they aim to increase F1 enrollment by 30% while maintaining a 90% retention rate. Tune in to hear more about their goals and initiatives!

snippet.title: The WAYS CX Framework
snippet.description:
The customer of the future is here. Is your company ready?

The last few years have caused consumers to re-evaluate their purpose and what they value in life. With new priorities, many customers are changing brands and thinking differently about their relationships with brands. 

Customer experience is a decision everyone in your company needs to make every day. When you invest in making your customers’ lives easier and better, you set your company up for long-term success and loyalty.

Creating a customer-centric mindset means deciding every day to put customers first. There are four ways to do that, which follow my WAYS framework.

snippet.title: Lillian Gish (1893-1993)
snippet.description: Lillian Gish was an American actress known as the "First Lady of American Cinema." She had a prolific career spanning over seven decades and appeared in numerous silent and sound films, earning acclaim for her expressive performances and contributions to the art of filmmaking.

snippet.title: Old-Time TOTW #279: Hop High Ladies (W. S. Collins) 10/29/23 #fiddle
snippet.description:
Old-Time TOTW #279 is Hop High Ladies from the playing of Willie Stephen"W. S." Collins (26 November 1878--8 April 1965) of Shawnee, Pottawatomie County, OK. He was the son of Bartholomew Collins (1854-1946) and Mary Ann "Polly" Capps (1865-1922). His ancestor John Richard Collins (b. 1655, Cornwall, England) was in Virginia by 1675. (source for above, Collins Family tree and public documents).

W.S. Collins was born in Missouri and moved to Wynnewood, Oklahoma in 1917. He finally settled in Shawnee, OK (Pottawatomie Co.) with is family. Collins came from a very musical family. In addition to siblings who played the fiddle, Willie's sons Earl and Max played fiddle as well. Earl Collins mentioned in an interview, "We could have had a family like the Carter Family. There was four girls and five boys [Earl's siblings], and every one of them musicians. The girls...had guitars and sang." Earl also mentioned that W. S.'s father and grandfather fiddled as well. 

Marion Thede collected many tunes from W. S. Collins as early as the 1930s and included a great deal of them in The Fiddle Book which was published in 1967. Many of the tunes she collected from other fiddlers were ones that the Collins family also played. 

About the tune, Thede states:

"In some of our instrumental folk tunes the fiddler adds to the delivery not only by simultaneously playing and singing, but also by laughing or shouting with the music. Shouting in a nasal falsetto is used in Hop High Ladies. Mr. Collins also sand the words, "hop high ladies" in falsetto in the places indicated in the second section, continuing to play the music."

This is a very different version of the tune, and it is not related to others which are based on the Irish tune Miss McLeod's Reel. 

Joining me are friends Stephen Rapp on banjo (Kent, OH) and Michael Seiser on guitar (Cleveland, OH)

Paul Kirk has been playing bowed string instruments since 1980 and has been teaching them privately, in classroom settings, and in workshop formats since 1988. He studied music theory, composition, music education, music history, and musicology at Cleveland State University. While at CSU, Paul played violas da gamba, krummhorns, and recorders in early music ensembles. He was a founding member of Good Company: A Vocal Ensemble and has founded many of his own bands over the years including A Hundred Thousand Welcomes Celtic Ensemble, Knives and Forks English Country Dance Ensemble, and Down the Road Old-Time String Band. He has composed over 500 folk tunes in a variety of styles. 

Paul has closely studied the music from  Appalachia, the history of American old-time fiddling, source fiddlers, as well as the social aspects of the music. Since July 1, 2018, he has been creating and sharing videos each Sunday through his Old-Time Tune of the Week series on YouTube. Paul puts a great focus on fiddle bowing patterns and techniques and has devised practical methods for teaching with excellent results. One of his specialties is teaching old-time fiddle to crossover students, especially those coming from a background in classical violin and other musical traditions.   

Subscribe to Paul's Patreon: www.patreon.com/paulkirk 
Join the Old-Time TOTW group on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/groups/33100...

In 2022, Paul was designated as a master old-time fiddler and received a Traditional Arts grant from the Ohio Arts Council to work with an apprentice of his choice. 

Paul taught viola, choir, music history, and assisted with drama and art for ten consecutive summers at orchestra and fine arts camps for The Music Settlement when it was known as Cleveland Music School Settlement.

Subscribe to Stephen Rapp's YouTube channel: https://www.youtube.com/user/rappstephen

snippet.title: Lindsey Wade solves a decades-old kidnapping cold case!
snippet.description:
Today Retired Detective Lindsey Wade shares the story of solving a decades-old kidnapping cold case.

In 1986, a young girl took a bike ride through Point Defiance Park near Tacoma, Washington. She never returned home. 

Investigators conducted a desperate search but came up empty handed. 

The case went dormant for decades and eventually ended up on the desk of cold-case detective Lindsey Wade. Through the application of emerging DNA testing and hard work, Wade and her fellow detectives were finally able to piece together what happened that day and bring closure to the family.

Small Town Dicks Podcast
Season 13 | Episode 1
At Last

Guest detective: Lindsey Wade
Lindsey served as a Tacoma Police Officer for twenty-one years. During her fourteen years as a detective, she investigated sexual assaults, child abuse, missing persons, and homicides. In 2010, Lindsey discovered that serial killer Ted Bundy’s DNA was not in CODIS. She worked with authorities in Florida to track down a sample of Bundy’s DNA and finally entered it into the national database in 2011. In 2012, Lindsey’s work on collecting DNA from convicted sexual predators in Washington state who’d slipped through the cracks led to an arrest in the 1980 homicide of a teenage girl. Lindsey retired in 2018 as the Tacoma Police Department’s cold case detective and joined the Washington State Attorney General’s Office as a senior investigator assigned for the Sexual Assault Kit Initiative. She is a former member of the FBI ViCAP National Advisory Board and teaches child abduction response and cold case investigations for the National Criminal Justice Training Center at Fox Valley Technical College. Lindsey has been a speaker at numerous law enforcement conferences around the country, lecturing on cold cases, sex crimes, DNA, and child abduction response. She recently published a true crime memoir titled, “In My DNA: My Career Investigating Your Worst Nightmares”.
